fields.label: Treatment
fields.options:
Passthrough
Subassembly Existing
Subassembly New
Other
fields.description: Defaults from the framework's Node Type when the framework is loaded; override per row if this mapping needs to deviate from the template. "Other" is set automatically on rows discovered by exploding a selected BOM — it isn't meant to be picked by hand.

fields.label: BOM
fields.description: Pick an existing BOM to reuse it (its raw materials get exploded and diffed against the framework) — filtered to BOMs for this row's Item Code. Leave blank on a Subassembly New row and use the "Generate BOMs" button to build one from this mapping's own children. If "Allow Editing Components" below is checked and the components are edited, clicking "Generate BOMs" builds a brand-new BOM in parallel and repoints this field to it — the originally selected BOM here is never modified.

fields.label: Allow Editing Components
fields.description: Unlocks Type / Treatment / Item Code / Qty on this row's "Other" components (the rows added by exploding the selected BOM above) so they can be edited or even turned into their own Subassembly Existing/New rows. One-way: once checked, this box locks itself — there's no unchecking it back. Edits only take effect on the BOM once you click "Generate BOMs", which builds a new BOM in parallel and repoints this row's BOM field to it.

fields.label: Not In Framework
fields.description: Set automatically when this row was added by exploding a selected existing BOM and has no matching row in the framework. Informational only — never written back to the framework.

fields.label: BOM Source Row
fields.description: Internal: the row name whose BOM selection produced this extra row, so re-selecting a different BOM only clears the extras tied to that row.

fields.label: Components Editable
fields.description: Internal: set on a row when its parent's "Allow Editing Components" checkbox is checked. Governs whether this row's normally-locked fields (Type, Treatment, Item Code, Qty) are currently editable.
